target.name: AIR
target.founder: Rani Plaut, Chen Rosen, Netanel Goldberg

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
AIR is a Series A transportation company that has raised $23M to date. The comparable universe consists of 8 private transportation firms including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger; no public transportation comps were identified as sufficiently analogous.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $23M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $46M. This method is appropriate for early-stage private companies where fundraising history serves as the most reliable anchor. Peer-median analysis was not applicable; while 5 same-sector peers exist, none yielded a sufficiently dense narrow peer set (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier), with only 1 same-stage comparable identified. Broadening to all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given transportation's wide valuation dispersion across business models and technology maturity.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $39M to $53M, with a median of $46M. This range reflects the stage-progression method as the binding constraint. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.0x multiplier for this specific sub-sector, AIR's runway and burn profile relative to peers, and unobserved product-market fit metrics. Additional data on unit economics, customer concentration, or comparable recent Series A transactions in frontier transportation would narrow this range materially.
